name: Ginger Fish Rolls
recipeIngredient: 213 g canned Alaska salmon (pink or red)
8 large Chinese leaves, blanched to soften
4 spring onions, trimmed and cut into 2.5cm lengths
1/2 tsp fresh root ginger, finely minced
1 tsp fine shreds of lime rind
recipeInstructions:
1. If you do not have a bamboo steamer, use a colander placed over a saucepan and covered with a lid.
2. Drain the can of salmon, reserving the juice.
3. Flake the fish coarsely.
4. Flatten the Chinese leaves. Trim away any tough stalks.
5. Place equal amounts of salmon onto the center of each leaf.
6. Surround with prawns, onions, ginger, and mushrooms.
7. Roll up, tucking ends in securely. Tie up with chives or raffia.
8. Place in the top of the steamer.
9. Mix soy sauce, lime juice, and lime rind.
10. Put rice into the base of the steamer with water and salmon juice.
11. Cover with fish rolls in the steamer top and lid.
12. Simmer for 20 minutes until most of the liquid is absorbed. Keep fish rolls warm.
13. Drain rice of any excess water and press into 12 small molds.
14. Turn onto a serving plate and garnish with lumpfish caviar.
15. Serve with the warm fish rolls and sauce.
Serves 4. Approx. 195 kcals per serving.
